metaTitle: Accessible Online Table Builder — WCAG Compliant | {{BRAND_NAME}}
metaDescription: {{BRAND_NAME}} implements the WAI-ARIA grid pattern, keyboard navigation, and screen reader announcements. Build tables everyone can use with confidence.
heroSubtext: Built-in WAI-ARIA grid pattern, full keyboard navigation, and screen reader announcements make {{BRAND_NAME}}'s editor usable by people who rely on assistive technology.

benefits.heading: WAI-ARIA grid pattern
benefits.body: The table uses role="grid", role="row", role="gridcell", aria-rowindex, and aria-colindex. Screen readers navigate the table correctly — not as a flat block of text.

benefits.heading: Full keyboard navigation
benefits.body: Navigate cells with Arrow keys, Tab, and Shift+Tab. Enter edit mode with Enter, exit with Escape. No mouse required for any table operation.

benefits.heading: Screen reader announcements
benefits.body: Structural changes (row added, cells merged) are announced via aria-live="polite" regions. Users of assistive technology always know what changed.

benefits.heading: Colour-independent selection
benefits.body: Selected cells show a ring indicator (ring-2 ring-primary) in addition to colour. Color swatches use aria-pressed and checkmark icons — never colour alone.

steps.heading: Navigate by keyboard
steps.body: Use Arrow keys to move between cells. Tab moves to the next cell, Shift+Tab moves backward. The focused cell has a visible ring.

steps.heading: Edit with screen reader on
steps.body: Tablesmit announces row and column counts, cell positions, and structural changes. Merged cells report aria-colspan and aria-rowspan for accurate navigation.

steps.heading: Export accessible formats
steps.body: HTML export preserves ARIA attributes. PDF export includes tagged table structure. LaTeX export produces accessible tabular environments.

useCases: Building tables that must meet WCAG 2.1 AA compliance standards
Creating government and public sector documents with accessibility requirements
Publishing data tables on websites that serve users of assistive technology
Developing educational materials for inclusive classroom environments
Ensuring research data is accessible to all readers regardless of ability
